fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Utah CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Utah - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity utah, how to transfer CNA license to utah, utah CNA endorsement application

board.name: Utah DOPL
board.mailing_address:
Utah Department of Commerce
Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 146741
Salt Lake City, Utah 84114-6741

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Utah.

steps.title: Complete Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Utah Department of Commerce, Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL) approved Nurse Aide Training and Competency Evaluation Program (NATCEP).

steps.title: Pass the NNAAP Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass both the written/oral and skills portions of the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) examination, administered by Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Create Utah.gov Account
steps.description: Navigate to the DOPL Online Services portal and create a my.utah.gov account if you don't have one, or log in using your existing credentials.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Access the 'New Application' section within the DOPL Online Portal, select 'Nurse Aide' as the application type, and complete all required fields and declarations.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $40.00 through the DOPL Online Portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Verify Application Status
steps.description: Monitor the status of your application and any requests for additional information through your my.utah.gov account on the DOPL Online Portal.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Utah CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $50. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Utah.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in UT.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Utah.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Utah, applicants must complete approved training, pass the NNAAP exam, and then submit their application through the DOPL Online Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Utah
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Utah CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DOPL Online Services portal, click on 'New Application'. You will be redirected to the Utah.gov login page. Create a my.utah.gov account if you don't have one, or log in with your existing account. Once logged in, select 'Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL)' and then choose the 'Nurse Aide' application type to begin.
